description_long: This is a tall, narrow apartment building with a steeply pitched, brown-tiled gable roof and a timber-framed facade reminiscent of traditional European architecture. The front features a symmetrical arrangement of windows with wooden frames, decorative crossbeams, and a prominent wooden entrance at street level, flanked by shopfront windows. The sides and rear of the building are plain, with smooth, light gray plastered walls and minimal window openings, emphasizing the utilitarian nature of these elevations. The building appears to have three main floors plus an attic, with the upper floors set back slightly under the roof eaves. The overall structure is vertically oriented, with a strong emphasis on height and narrowness, typical of historic urban residential buildings in dense city centers. The materials and design suggest a sturdy, long-lasting construction suitable for housing multiple families or tenants.
description: A tall, narrow, timber-framed apartment building with a steep gable roof, decorative front facade, and plain plastered sides and rear. The front features symmetrical windows and a wooden entrance at street level.
description_view_0: The front view shows the decorative timber-framed facade, symmetrical windows, and a prominent wooden entrance with shopfront windows at the base.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, light gray plastered wall with no windows or decorative features, and the overhanging tiled roof.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a simple, unadorned wall with a few small windows and a section of the roof extending over a lower rear portion.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, with a plain plastered wall and no visible windows or ornamentation.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged on the front facade in horizontal rows, with evenly spaced groupings on each floor, while the sides and rear have minimal and irregularly placed windows.
